relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Saul approves the death as persecution scatters the Jerusalem assembly.
Stephen is buried with deep lament.
Saul ravages homes and imprisons men and women.
Scattering becomes seed for good news.
Philip proclaims Messiah in Samaria.
The crowds attend with one accord because word and signs meet.
Unclean spirits are released and bodies are healed.
Great joy fills the city.
Simon had amazed Samaria by magic and self-greatness.
The people had named him great power.
Long amazement had bound attention to him.
Philip’s good news turns attention to the reign and name of Jesus Messiah.
Simon trusts and follows, still amazed by power.
Jerusalem hears Samaria has received the word.
Peter and John pray for holy Spirit to be received.
They had been baptized into the name, but Spirit had not yet fallen on them.
Hands are laid and holy Spirit is received.
Simon sees Spirit-gift through hands and brings silver.
He wants authority as purchasable power.
Peter rejects money as a way to acquire divine gift.
Simon’s heart is not straight in the word.
Turning and prayer are offered as the way out of harmful intention.
Peter sees bitterness and injustice binding him.
Simon asks them to pray that the spoken warning not come.
Witness continues through Samaritan villages.
A messenger sends Philip to the wilderness road.
An Ethiopian eunuch, royal treasurer and worshiper, returns from Jerusalem.
He reads Isaiah in his chariot.
The Spirit tells Philip to join the chariot.
Philip asks whether the reader understands.
The seeker asks for guidance and makes room beside him.
The Scripture speaks of sheeplike suffering and silence.
Humiliation and denied justice become the passage.
The eunuch asks whom the prophet means.
From Isaiah, Philip announces Jesus.
Water appears, and the eunuch asks what prevents baptism.
A later manuscript tradition adds a confession before baptism.
Both go down into the water, and the eunuch is baptized.
Spirit snatches Philip away, and the eunuch continues rejoicing.
Philip is found at Azotus and keeps announcing good news to Caesarea.
